summary: MAMSI is a Python framework designed for the integration of multi-assay mass spectrometry datasets.

MAMSI provides a platform for linking statistically significant features of untargeted multi-assay LC-MS metabolomics datasets into clusters defined by their structural properties based on mass-to-charge ratio and retention time.
The MAMSI framework was tested on metabolomics phenotyping data but should be usable with other types of LC-MS data.
MamsiPls is a class that extends the MB_PLS class by extra methods convenient in Chemometrics and Metabolomics research.
MamsiPls is based on the MB-PLS package by Baum et al., 2019.
The default number of latent variables (LV) for MamsiPls is 2.
The default method used to derive model attributes in MamsiPls is NIPALS.
The default maximum tolerance for the iterative NIPALS algorithm in MamsiPls is 1e-14.
The default nipals_convergence_norm for MamsiPls is 2.
MamsiPls defaults to standardizing the data using unit-variance scaling.
The estimate_lv method in MamsiPls estimates the number of latent variables using cross-validation combined with an outer loop with increasing number of LVs.
The default maximum number of components for LV estimation in MamsiPls is 10.
The default metric for LV estimation in MamsiPls for classification is AUC.

The default cross-validation method for LV estimation in MamsiPls is k-fold. The default number of splits for k-fold cross-validation in MamsiPls is 5. Available cross-validation methods for LV estimation in MamsiPls are k-fold and Monte Ca

Available metrics for LV estimation in MamsiPls for categorical outcome variables are AUC, precision, recall, and f1. The available metric for LV estimation in MamsiPls for continuous outcome variables is q2.
The default plateau threshold for LV estimation in MamsiPls is 0.01. The default increase threshold for LV estimation in MamsiPls is 0.05.
The evaluate_class_model method evaluates a classification MB-PLS model using a testing dataset. The evaluate_regression_model method evaluates a regression MB-PLS model using a testing dataset.

The mb_vip method calculates Multi-block Variable Importance in Projection scores for the multiblock PLS model. The block_importance method calculates the block importance for each block in the multiblock PLS model.
The mb_vip_permtest method calculates empirical p-values for each feature by permuting the Y outcome variable and refitting the model.
The default number of permutations for the mb_vip_permtest method is 1000.
The calculate_ci static method calculates mean, margin of error, and confidence interval for each column.
The default confidence level for the calculate_ci method is 0.90.
The group_train_test_split static method splits data into train and test sets based on groups.
MamsiStructSearch is a class for performing structural search on multi-modal MS data.
MamsiStructSearch allows searching for structural signatures in LC-MS data based on m/z and RT, including isotopologues and adduct patterns.
The default retention time tolerance window for MamsiStructSearch is 5.
The default mass-to-charge ratio tolerance for MamsiStructSearch is 15 ppm.

The load_msi method of MamsiStructSearch imports MSI intensity data and extracts feature metadata from column names. The load_lcms method of MamsiStructSearch imports LC-MS intensity data and extracts feature metadata from column names.
The get_structural_clusters method searches structural signatures in LC-MS data including isotopologues, adduct patterns, and cross-assay links.
The default adduct option for the get_structural_clusters method is 'all'. The default annotate option for the get_structural_clusters method is True.
The get_correlation_clusters method clusters features based on their correlations using hierarchical clustering.

The default flat_method for the get_correlation_clusters method is 'constant'. The default cut_threshold for the get_correlation_clusters method is 0.7. The default max_clusters for the get_correlation_clusters method is 5.
The get_structural_network method generates a structural network graph based on structural links data or a provided master file.
MAMSI can be installed from PyPI using pip.
MAMSI requires Python version 3.9 or higher.
The AddNeuroMed dataset comprises 283 male and 294 female samples.
The HPOS assay has 681 features.
The LPOS assay has 4,886 features.
The LNEG assay has 2,091 features.
MB-PLS algorithm is based on the mbpls package version 1.0.4.
